events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, lead singer of The Rolling Stones, was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent, England.

angles.name: Rock Legend Birthday
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still commanding stages! Happy Birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger, frontman of The Rolling Stones. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched 45 years ago
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) first launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the first music video ever aired.

angles.name: MTV Anniversary
angles.copy: 45 years since MTV changed the game! 'Video Killed The Radio Star' was the first track played, but did it really? What impact did MTV have on your music taste?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' anniversary
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring singles like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: Twenty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and kicked off a Britpop revolution. Which track still gives you goosebumps?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began 57 years ago
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music and Art Fair, '3 Days of Peace & Music', took place from August 15-18, 1969, on a dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Woodstock Anniversary
angles.copy: Rewind to 1969: Woodstock began, a festival that defined a generation with three days of peace, love, and music. Which iconic performance do you wish you'd seen?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's passing
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977. He would have been 91 this year, having been born in 1935.

angles.name: Elvis Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the King today. Elvis Presley's influence on music is immeasurable, 49 years after his passing. What's your all-time favourite Elvis hit?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's passing
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, an influential rapper, was shot in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and died on September 13, 1996. He would have been 55 this year, having been born in 1971.

angles.name: Tupac Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur, a voice that defined a generation and continues to resonate. His music and legacy live on.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's passing
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the most influential electric guitarists in music history, passed away on September 18, 1970. He would have been 84 this year, having been born in 1942.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled talent of Jimi Hendrix, whose groundbreaking guitar work continues to inspire. A true legend gone too soon.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on September 26, 1969, featuring iconic tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun'.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still one of the most recognisable in music history. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, lead vocalist of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio, USA.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the iconic Chrissie Hynde! The Pretenders frontwoman still rocks harder than most. What's your go-to Pretenders song?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, known as Liam Gallagher, lead singer of Oasis, was born on September 21, 1972, in Burnage, Manchester, England.

angles.name: Liam Gallagher Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation, still bringing the rock 'n' roll swagger. What's your favourite Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released on October 11, 1971, with the album of the same name following in September 1971.

angles.name: Imagine Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon released 'Imagine', a timeless anthem for peace and unity. Its message still resonates today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: First iPod released 25 years ago
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music players.

angles.name: iPod Anniversary
angles.copy: Remember your first iPod? 25 years ago, Apple launched the device that changed how we listen to music forever. How many songs did you cram onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', on October 5, 1962.

angles.name: James Bond Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From iconic gadgets to unforgettable theme tunes, who is your favourite 007 and what's the best Bond film?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles, was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool, England.

angles.name: John Lennon Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to inspire generations.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: John Marwood Cleese, the renowned English actor, comedian, and writer, was born on October 27, 1939, in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, England.

angles.name: John Cleese Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 87th Birthday to the legend that is John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, his comedic genius is unmatched. What's your favourite Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles' 'White Album' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years since The Beatles delivered their sprawling masterpiece, 'The White Album'. A diverse collection that showed every facet of the Fab Four. Which track stands out for you?
